channel.title: NYT > World News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Iran Escalates Retaliatory Strikes as U.S. Signals Long Battle
item.description: Iranian strikes on the U.A.E. were reported early Tuesday, the U.S. Embassy in Riyadh was hit by drones, and Israel said it was attacking Hezbollah targets in Lebanon. President Trump said the campaign could last weeks or more.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: As Trump Bombs Iran, America’s Allies Watch Fitfully From Sidelines
item.description: Disregarded by President Trump over Iran, Europe’s leaders are adapting to a world in which they are little more than bystanders.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Strikes on Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Pakistan Strikes Bagram Air Base, Satellite Images Show
item.description: Satellite imagery showed flattened warehouses at the base that served as the nerve center of America’s 20-year war in Afghanistan, a prized asset for the Taliban.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier, Christiaan Triebert and Yaqoob Akbary

item.title: After Attacks in Iran, Cubans Worry They Might Be Trump’s Next Target
item.description: Many Cubans are wondering if the Trump administration plans to target their country’s Communist government next.
item.dc:creator: Ed Augustin, Frances Robles and David C. Adams
item.media:description: A line of cars waits outside a gas station in Havana last month. Cuba is suffering from a severe energy shortage largely brought on by a U.S. blockade of foreign oil shipments,

item.title: How the Bombing of Iran Is Affecting Lebanon, Kuwait and Other Countries
item.description: The Middle East is facing deaths and destruction as Iran retaliates against a huge American-Israeli military campaign.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smoke rising from an Israeli air attack that struck suburbs south of Beirut, Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: Hezbollah Plunges Lebanon Back Into War Despite Its Weakness
item.description: The militant group’s attacks, apparently at the behest of Iran, led to retaliation from Israel and were “practically a suicide mission” for Hezbollah, an analyst said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Hezbollah supporters rallied in Beirut, Lebanon, on Sunday in solidarity with Iran after U.S. and Israeli strikes killed Iran’s supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Carney Hails New Ties Between Canada and India, Setting Aside Rift Over Killing
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney focused on business during a meeting with his Indian counterpart, Narendra Modi, with little mention of India’s alleged role in a deadly shooting in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada, left, with the Indian leader, Narendra Modi, in New Delhi on Monday.

item.title: Macron Expands French Nuclear Arsenal and Vows Protection for Neighbors
item.description: The announcement was a significant step in forging a European deterrent as Russia grows more aggressive and the U.S. steps back.
item.dc:creator: Mark Landler
item.category: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:credit: Pool Photo by Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: President Emmanuel Macron of France speaking before a nuclear-powered submarine at a navy base in Crozon, France, on Monday.

item.title: Bagram Air Base Targeted by Pakistan, Afghanistan Says
item.description: Afghan officials said they had thwarted a Pakistani airstrike on the former U.S. base, Bagram airfield, amid an intensifying campaign that has targeted dozens of military sites across the country.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier and Safiullah Padshah
item.media:credit: David Guttenfelder for The New York Times
item.media:description: Taliban fighters at the Bagram air field in Afghanistan in 2021.

item.title: ‘It’s Surreal’: Iranians in Disbelief After Supreme Leader Is Killed
item.description: Large crowds of people celebrated the killing of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in American and Israeli strikes, while many others gathered to mourn.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Kiana Hayeri
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A rally to mourn Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the supreme leader of Iran, in Tehran on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Attacks Dubai, Abu Dhabi, Qatar and Bahrain After U.S.-Israeli Strikes
item.description: More than 100 people were injured and at least four were killed after Iran fired hundreds of missiles and drones at Gulf cities, including Dubai, Abu Dhabi and Doha.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim and Omnia Al Desoukie
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: A plume of smoke from an Iranian strike rising in the distance from the Dubai International Airport.

item.title: After Attack on Iran by the U.S. and Israel, See Photos and Videos From the Middle East
item.description: Here are images from Iran, Israel, Lebanon and elsewhere in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: An Israeli strike on Beirut, Lebanon, on Monday.

item.title: China’s Parents Are Outsourcing the Homework Grind to A.I.
item.description: Parents in China are turning to A.I. chatbots and other tools to help their children gain an edge and ease the fighting over homework.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Wang and Jiawei Wang

item.title: The U.S. Released the Epstein Files. The Arrests Are Overseas.
item.description: While the latest tranche of documents about the sex offender Jeffrey Epstein has exposed a deeply American scandal, it is Europe where the aggressive enforcement is taking place.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:description: Police officers at the entrance to Wood Farm on the Sandringham Estate in Norfolk, Britain, last week, where Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor is currently based.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Helene Cooper and Yan Zhuang

item.title: U.S. Attacks on Iran Test Fragile Truce With China
item.description: Beijing has condemned the U.S.-backed strikes on Iran, a close partner. Yet with trade talks looming, it is unlikely to risk a rupture with Washington.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, right, and the country’s top diplomat, Wang Yi, in Beijing last month.

item.title: As Trump Scrambles the World Order, Can Germany Learn the Language of Hard Power?
item.description: In the wake of the U.S. bombing of Iran and its dismissal of European allies, an anxious continent’s best chance at security runs through its largest economy.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Zerofsky

item.title: When Trump Meets Germany’s Merz, Iran Will Likely Dominate Discussion
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s visit to Washington, long planned, is now likely to be dominated by discussions of the attack, which Europeans did not take part in.
item.dc:creator: Jim Tankersley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz of Germany, left, with President Trump in October.

item.title: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
item.description: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.
item.dc:creator: Saikou Jammeh, Ruth Maclean, Dickson Adama and Ismail Auwal
item.media:description: Razed homes are seen days after an attack that left dozens dead in the Muslim-majority village of Woro, Nigeria, last month.

item.title: In Myanmar, Junta Leader Eyes the Title of President
item.description: The military ruler of Myanmar, Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, is expected to seek a civilian post to add a veneer of legitimacy after sham elections.
item.dc:creator: Richard C. Paddock
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
item.media:description: Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing leaving a polling station in Naypyidaw, Myanmar, in December.

item.title: Global Economy Is Facing the Prospect of Another Profound Shock
item.description: A protracted conflict in the Middle East risks a spike in energy prices and broader inflation.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Firefighters at the Ras Tanura oil refinery in Saudi Arabia on Monday, after a drone attack. The Middle East is the source of 30 percent of the world’s oil.

item.title: ‘Pray We Make It Through the Night’: Iran’s Capital Under Siege
item.description: Many residents of Tehran managed to get out of town when the U.S. and Israel attack began, but others who could not described living under bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Arash Khamooshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Destruction of Cultural Antiquities and Heritage
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People are standing behind a yellow line after a police station on Niloufar street was hit by US-Israel attacks and damaged the next door buildings in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Under Pressure from Trump, Cuban Leader Calls for ‘Urgent’ Economic Change
item.description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel said the private sector needed more autonomy, as the island confronts a U.S. oil blockade that has deepened a humanitarian crisis.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and David C. Adams
item.media:description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel of Cuba, center, at a funeral in January for those killed in U.S. strikes on Venezuela.

item.title: U.S. Embassy in Saudi Capital Hit by 2 Drones on Tuesday, Kingdom Says
item.description: Americans in Riyadh and other cities were advised to shelter in place immediately.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Brooks Kraft LLC/Corbis, via Getty Images
item.media:description: The United States Embassy in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia.

item.title: Trump Embraces U.S. Military Power After Years of Caution
item.description: The wars in Afghanistan and Iraq offered a stark lesson in the limits of military force. The Iran attacks suggest an era of postwar wariness is over.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump in Corpus Christi, Texas, last week. His sudden embrace of U.S. military power is a stunning reversal.

item.title: Why Eliminating Iran’s Ballistic Missiles Could Be Difficult
item.description: Much of Tehran’s arsenal of missiles may be hidden underground, making it harder to destroy.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

item.title: Can Trump Legally Kill Iran’s Leader?
item.description: The United States and Israel started a war with Iran by killing its supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman holding a photo of Ayatollah Khamenei during a rally in Tehran last month.

item.title: U.S. Slaps Sanctions on Rwanda, Saying It Sabotaged Trump Peace Deal
item.description: Rwanda’s government responded by claiming the sanctions unjustly targeted only one party to the conflict and misrepresented the facts.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:description: President Trump, President Paul Kagame of Rwanda, center, and President Felix Tshisekedi of the Democratic Republic of Congo participate in a signing ceremony at the Institute of Peace in Washington in December 2025.

item.title: Trump Signals U.S. Is Prepared for a Long War Against Iran
item.description: President Trump also declined to rule out sending ground troops into Iran as the conflict spread into the Gulf countries and Lebanon and Israel exchanged strikes.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: People gathering their belongings at their damaged homes on Monday after U.S.-Israeli airstrikes hit a police station in Tehran. President Trump said that still-bigger waves of airstrikes were coming.

item.title: Rubio Warns Iran Faces ‘Hardest Hits’ Ahead, Offering Conflicting Rationales
item.description: The secretary of state signaled that an escalation in Iran was likely and said the attacks underway were both pre-emptive and defensive, describing a far more extensive mission than was initially laid out.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters on Capitol Hill on Monday that the “next phase will be even more punishing on Iran than it is right now.”

item.title: U.S. University Campuses in Mideast Move Classes Online as War Spreads
item.description: A number of major universities with campuses in Qatar and other nations are operating remotely as the conflict expands throughout the region.
item.dc:creator: Mark Arsenault and Alan Blinder
item.media:description: Iran has struck targets in Doha, Qatar. Several U.S. universities have campuses in the area.

item.title: A top Iranian official pledges attacks on tankers looking to pass through Strait of Hormuz.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis and Parin Behrooz
item.media:description: An aerial view of the Iranian shores and the island of Qeshm in the Strait of Hormuz, in 2023.

item.title: U.S. Military Death Toll in Iran War Rises to 6
item.description: The number of U.S. service members killed in the first three days of the war grew as officials said the remains of two more troops had been recovered.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Gen. Dan Caine, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, during a Medal of Honor ceremony at the White House on Monday. The general said he expected the United States to “take additional losses.”

item.title: Conflict Spreads Across the Gulf
item.description: Iran is expanding the conflict to put pressure on the U.S. and its allies.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Damage from U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in Tehran yesterday.

item.title: Attack on U.S.-Flagged Oil Tanker in Bahrain Kills Shipyard Worker
item.description: It is not clear who attacked the tanker, Stena Imperative. Its U.S. operator, Crowley, described “aerial impacts” and said that a fire on board was quickly extinguished.
item.dc:creator: Christiaan Triebert and Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The Stena Imperative oil tanker in Quebec in August 2023. The ship was attacked at port in Bahrain on Monday.

item.title: As Maduro and Khamenei Learned, It’s Harder Than Ever for Leaders to Hide
item.description: A surge in sensors and cameras, combined with artificial intelligence, has transformed U.S. intelligence’s ability to locate foreign heads of state. Add to that an American president willing to capture or kill them.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Wiretapping and Other Eavesdropping Devices and Methods
item.media:description: Nicolás Maduro was captured by U.S. forces in January after American intelligence was able to locate him at an apartment inside a Caracas military base.

item.title: Palestinian Brothers Killed as Settler Violence Surges in the West Bank
item.description: Israeli extremists appear to have escalated their campaign to take over more of the occupied lands as world attention shifts to the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Kershner and Fatima AbdulKarim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Friends and family grieved near the bodies of two Palestinian brothers who were fatally shot on Monday.

item.title: Qatar Says It Shot Down Two Iranian Bombers
item.description: It was the first report in this conflict of Iran attempting to attack its Persian Gulf neighbors with warplanes. The incident indicates a sharp escalation in tensions between Iran and Qatar.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rose from an area in the direction of Al Udeid Air Base on Saturday, where usually thousands of American soldiers are stationed.

item.title: Satellite Images Show Burning Iranian Naval Ships
item.description: Smoke billows from at least four military ships that have burned for more than 24 hours, according to images released on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Christoph Koettl
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: How Trump Decided to Go to War With Iran
item.description: President Trump’s embrace of military action in Iran was spurred by an Israeli leader determined to end diplomatic negotiations. Few of the president’s advisers voiced opposition.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Edward Wong, Eric Schmitt and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times

item.title: Dubai Says ‘Limited’ Flights Have Resumed From Its Airports
item.description: Emirates and FlyDubai said they were making a small number of flights to and from Dubai starting Monday night after shutting down because of airstrikes in the region.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross and Ismaeel Naar

item.title: The Unexpected Winners From Trump’s New Global Tariff
item.description: The president’s flat 10 percent tariff is most beneficial to nations that previously faced the highest rates. But it’s not clear how much that will prompt a new surge in imports.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Lazaro Gamio

item.title: Spain Denies U.S. Military Use of Its Bases for Iran Attack
item.description: The leftist government in Madrid said the war against Iran violated both international law and the agreement between Spain and the United States on the use of air bases.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: “Spanish military bases will not be used for anything that falls outside the agreement with the United States and the United Nations Charter,” Spain’s foreign minister said.

item.title: Behind Trump’s Reversal On Regime Change
item.description: As a candidate, Donald J. Trump criticized regime change as “a proven, absolute failure.” Now he finds himself pursuing the exact kind of regime change he once criticized. Our chief White House correspondent, Peter Baker, tracks the president’s evolution on this issue.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker, Coleman Lowndes, Leila Medina, James Surdam and Alexandra Ostasiewicz

item.title: More Lebanese Flee Their Homes as Israel Strikes Back at Iran-Backed Hezbollah
item.description: The three-day U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and its allies brought a new wave of displacement to war-weary Lebanon, after Israel retaliated for Hezbollah rocket attacks.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and Hwaida Saad
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
item.media:description: Displaced Lebanese gather along the Mediterranean coast in Beirut on Monday after fleeing their homes. Iran-backed Hezbollah attacked Israel overnight, bringing heavy Israeli retaliation.

item.title: Iranian Strikes Hit Qatar and Saudi Energy Sites, Threatening Oil and Gas Supplies
item.description: Strikes on sites in Qatar and Saudi Arabia forced the closure of key production facilities and added to growing worries about global oil and gas supplies.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Nereim and Ismaeel Naar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rose from Saudi Arabia’s Ras Tanura refinery on Monday following an Iranian drone attack.

item.title: Iran’s Top Security Official Calls Trump’s War Aims ‘Delusional’ and Says Iran ‘Will Not Negotiate’
item.description: Ali Larijani, seen as a pragmatist who could negotiate with Washington, struck a defiant tone and vowed to fight on.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Ali Larijani, Iran’s top security official, at a news conference in 2024. He has accused President Trump of getting into a war that harmed U.S. interests, and vowed to fight on.

item.title: 3 U.S. Planes Are Shot Down in ‘Friendly Fire’ in Kuwait, U.S. Military Says
item.description: Separately, a drone attack hit the U.S. Embassy compound in the Persian Gulf state, as Iran continues to target American assets across the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Boxerman, Natan Odenheimer, Adam Rasgon, Malachy Browne and Amelia Nierenberg
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: After a Drone Strikes RAF Base in Cyprus, UK Risks Being Dragged Further Into Iran Conflict
item.description: The government of Prime Minister Keir Starmer risks being dragged deeper into the conflict, after allowing the U.S. military to use British bases for “defensive” purposes.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An aircraft leaving R.A.F. Akrotiri, a British military base in Cyprus, on Monday. The base was hit by a drone Sunday night.

item.title: After ‘Priceless’ Violin Flies on Lap, Lufthansa Changes Carry-On Rules
item.description: Carolin Widmann had to cradle her centuries-old, multimillion-dollar instrument during a flight to Germany. Her predicament resonated with musicians who have faced similar challenges.
item.dc:creator: Christine Hauser
item.media:description: Carolin Widmann was boarding a plane with a Guadagnini violin, made in 1782, when she was instructed to take it out of its case.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh, Tyler Pager, Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper and Richard Pérez-Peña

item.title: China’s New Study Buddy: The A.I. Chatbot
item.description: Parents in China are using homegrown chatbots like DeepSeek and Doubao as well as Google’s Gemini to help their children with homework and give them an academic edge.
item.dc:creator: Jiawei Wang

item.title: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Facilities in Dubai, Bahrain and Other Middle East Locations
item.description: A Times analysis of satellite imagery and verified videos shows Iran found vulnerabilities in U.S. locations in the region.
item.dc:creator: Devon Lum and Haley Willis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Israel Strikes Beirut After Hezbollah Attacks
item.description: Residents fled in panic as Israeli jets struck Hezbollah strongholds in response to a midnight barrage, an expansion of the war between Israel, the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Christina Goldbaum and Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Smoke rising in Beirut, Lebanon, early on Monday.

item.title: Trump Says Iran War Could Last Weeks and Gives Competing Visions of New Regime
item.description: In a brief interview, he said the country’s hardened military should simply surrender their weapons to the Iranian public.
item.dc:creator: Zolan Kanno-Youngs, David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump boarding Air Force One at Palm Beach International Airport in West Palm Beach, Fla., on Sunday. He spoke to The Times in a brief phone interview.

item.title: In New York, Fury, Anxiety and Joy Over the Attacks on Iran
item.description: As Mayor Zohran Mamdani assailed what he called a “catastrophic escalation” in Iran, some Iranian Americans worried about what comes next, while others celebrated.
item.dc:creator: Dodai Stewart
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani of New York said Americans did not support a “war of aggression” in Iran.

item.title: Iranian Strikes on Gulf States Reach the Hundreds
item.description: Iran has fired nearly 400 missiles and over 800 drones across the Persian Gulf in the past two days, according to government reports.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: A damaged building in Manama, Bahrain, on Sunday.

item.title: Strike on Girls’ School Kills at Least 175, Iranian State Media Says
item.description: Videos and images verified by The New York Times showed that at least half of the school was destroyed. It was not immediately clear why the school was hit, or which country’s forces had fired at it.
item.dc:creator: Malachy Browne, Ephrat Livni and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: In Turn From Iran Talks to War, U.S. Casts Doubt on Diplomacy
item.description: President Trump again deployed heavy force against a country with which he had been negotiating, continuing a pattern seen in previous attacks on Iran and Venezuela.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump, seen on Friday, has given little public explanation on why he cut off talks and attacked Iran.

item.title: What Now in Iran?
item.description: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is dead. Whoever takes his place will shape the Middle East’s future.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
